skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem: 4(x + 9) = 12(x − 11)(x + 9). What is the sum of the solutions to the given equation?
rationale: Subtract 4(x + 9) from both sides: 0 = 12(x − 11)(x + 9) − 4(x + 9). Factor out (x + 9): 0 = (x + 9)[12(x − 11) − 4] = (x + 9)(12x − 136). So x = −9 or 12x = 136, i.e. x = 34/3. The sum of the solutions is −9 + 34/3 = (−27 + 34)/3 = 7/3.
